openvas.name: OpenVAS
openvas.short_description: OpenVAS is Greenbone's open-source vulnerability scanner, offering authenticated and unauthenticated testing with a daily-updated detection feed.

features.description: Tests systems with or without credentials across many protocol types.
features.title: Authenticated and unauthenticated scanning

features.description: Supports a range of high-level and low-level internet and industrial protocols.
features.title: Industrial and internet protocol testing

features.description: Includes a programming language for writing custom vulnerability tests.
features.title: Internal scripting language

features.description: Pulls vulnerability detection tests from a regularly maintained feed updated daily.
features.title: Daily-updated feed

features.description: Includes tuning options for running scans at scale.
features.title: Large-scale scanning support

features.description: Serves as the scanning engine bundled with other open-source Greenbone modules.
features.title: Core of Greenbone Community Edition

pros: Fully open source with source code available on GitHub
Daily-updated vulnerability test feed
Supports both authenticated and unauthenticated scanning
Extensible through its own internal scripting language
Backed by Greenbone, which has maintained it since 2006
cons: Best used as part of the broader Greenbone Community Edition rather than standalone
Enterprise features and support require Greenbone's commercial products
Requires self-hosting and infrastructure to run at scale

faqs.answer: Yes, OpenVAS is open source and forms the core of the free Greenbone Community Edition.
faqs.question: Is OpenVAS free?

faqs.answer: Greenbone has developed and maintained OpenVAS since 2006.
faqs.question: Who maintains OpenVAS?

faqs.answer: OpenVAS supports both unauthenticated and authenticated testing.
faqs.question: Does OpenVAS require authentication to scan?

faqs.answer: OpenVAS pulls detection tests from a feed that is updated daily.
faqs.question: How often is OpenVAS's vulnerability feed updated?

faqs.answer: Yes, OpenVAS is integrated into Greenbone's commercial OPENVAS SCAN product family for enterprise use.
faqs.question: Is there a commercial version of OpenVAS?

hashicorp-vault.name: HashiCorp Vault
hashicorp-vault.short_description: HashiCorp Vault secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys.

features.description: Stores and versions static secrets accessible via UI, CLI, or HTTP API.
features.title: Key/Value Secrets Engine

features.description: Dynamically generates short-lived database credentials on demand.
features.title: Database Secrets Engine

features.description: Provides encryption as a service for data in transit without storing the data itself.
features.title: Transit Secrets Engine

features.description: An Enterprise-only engine that supports tokenization of sensitive data.
features.title: Transform Secrets Engine

features.description: Policy-driven authentication and authorization for humans, machines, and AI agents.
features.title: Identity-based access control

features.description: Generates, rotates, and revokes certificates on demand.
features.title: Certificate management

pros: Vault Community edition is free and open source.
Covers multiple secrets engines (Key/Value, Database, Transit) for varied use cases.
HCP Vault Dedicated offers a cloud-hosted, managed option with a free trial.
Backed by official certification programs (Vault Associate, Vault Operations Professional).
cons: Enterprise-only features, such as the Transform Secrets Engine, require a paid license.
Public pricing for Vault Enterprise and HCP Vault is not listed on the product or pricing pages.
Company founding year and headquarters are not published on the pages reviewed.

faqs.answer: Yes, Vault Community edition is free and open source; Vault Enterprise requires a valid paid license.
faqs.question: Is HashiCorp Vault free?

faqs.answer: Vault secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys via a UI, CLI, or HTTP API.
faqs.question: What does Vault do?

faqs.answer: Yes, HCP Vault Dedicated is referenced as offering a free trial option.
faqs.question: Does HCP Vault offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: It provides encryption as a service, letting Vault encrypt and decrypt data in transit without Vault storing the underlying data.
faqs.question: What is the Transit Secrets Engine?

features: Key/Value, Database, and Transit secrets engines

akeyless.name: Akeyless
akeyless.short_description: Akeyless is a SaaS platform for secrets management, machine identity security, PKI, and multi-cloud key management.

features.description: Centrally stores and dynamically delivers static, dynamic, and rotated secrets to applications and pipelines.
features.title: Secrets Management

features.description: Patented encryption technology that fragments cryptographic keys across regions.
features.title: Distributed Fragments Cryptography

features.description: Keeps secrets out of AI agent context while validating agent actions at runtime via Agentic Runtime Authority.
features.title: SecretlessAI

features.description: Issues, rotates, and manages SSH and PKI certificates, with CA integration.
features.title: Certificate Lifecycle Management

features.description: Manages and performs encryption operations using keys across multiple cloud providers.
features.title: Multi-cloud Key Management (KMS)

features.description: Provides centralized governance across multiple third-party vaults.
features.title: Universal Secrets Connector

features.description: Zero-trust, just-in-time access controls for privileged sessions.
features.title: Privileged Access Management

pros: Offers a Free plan covering multiple product modules, not just a single feature.
Combines secrets management, PAM, PKI, and KMS in one platform.
Holds compliance certifications including ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, FIPS 140-3, GDPR, and DORA.
Provides SDKs and integrations for CI/CD, Kubernetes, and identity providers.
cons: Free plan quotas are restrictive (e.g., up to 5 clients, 500 static secrets, 3-day audit log retention).
Enterprise plan pricing is not published and requires contacting sales for a custom quote.
Encryption/KMS free tier is capped at 1,000 transactions per day.

faqs.answer: Yes, Akeyless has a Free plan with limited quotas across modules, such as up to 5 clients, 500 static secrets, and 3-day audit log retention.
faqs.question: Does Akeyless offer a free plan?

faqs.answer: SecretlessAI is Akeyless's approach to keeping secrets out of AI agents' context while still validating and authorizing their actions at runtime.
faqs.question: What is SecretlessAI?

faqs.answer: No, the Enterprise plan offers customizable quotas and pricing that requires contacting Akeyless directly.
faqs.question: Is Akeyless Enterprise pricing public?

faqs.answer: Akeyless states it holds ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI-DSS, AICPA, CSA STAR Level One, FIPS 140-3, and DORA compliance.
faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Akeyless hold?

grype.name: Grype
grype.short_description: Grype is Anchore's open-source vulnerability scanner for container images, filesystems, and SBOMs across major OS and language package ecosystems.

features.description: Scans packages from Alpine, Debian, Ubuntu, RHEL, Oracle Linux, and Amazon Linux.
features.title: Broad OS ecosystem coverage

features.description: Detects vulnerabilities in Ruby, Java, JavaScript, Python, .NET, Go, PHP, and Rust dependencies.
features.title: Language package support

features.description: Works with Docker, OCI, and Singularity image formats.
features.title: Multiple image format support

features.description: Scans pre-generated SBOM files directly, including those produced by Syft.
features.title: SBOM input scanning

features.description: Uses EPSS scores, CISA KEV data, and risk scoring to help prioritize which vulnerabilities to fix first.
features.title: Risk-based prioritization

features.description: Filters and enhances scan results using VEX (Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange) data.
features.title: OpenVEX support

pros: Free and open source under Apache-2.0
Broad ecosystem support across OS packages and multiple programming languages
Vulnerability prioritization via EPSS and KEV reduces noise
Multiple install methods, including curl, Homebrew, Docker, Chocolatey, and MacPorts
Active GitHub community with 12.6k+ stars
cons: Command-line tool with no built-in graphical dashboard
Some workflows require pairing with a separate SBOM tool like Syft
No official managed or hosted service documented

faqs.answer: Yes, Grype is open source under the Apache-2.0 license, sponsored by Anchore.
faqs.question: Is Grype free?

faqs.answer: Grype scans container images, filesystems, and SBOM files for known vulnerabilities.
faqs.question: What can Grype scan?

faqs.answer: Yes, Grype can scan SBOM files directly, including CycloneDX and SPDX formats.
faqs.question: Does Grype support SBOM files?

faqs.answer: Grype can be installed via a curl script, Homebrew, Docker, Chocolatey, or MacPorts.
faqs.question: How is Grype installed?

faqs.answer: Yes, it uses EPSS scores, CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list, and risk scoring to help prioritize remediation.
faqs.question: Does Grype prioritize vulnerabilities?

checkmarx.name: Checkmarx
checkmarx.short_description: Checkmarx One is an AI-powered application security platform offering SAST, DAST, SCA, and cloud-native security scanning for enterprise development teams.

features.description: Static application security testing that analyzes source code to find vulnerabilities during development.
features.title: NG SAST

features.description: Dynamic application security testing that assesses running applications for exploitable vulnerabilities.
features.title: DAST

features.description: Scans open-source dependencies for known vulnerabilities and license risks.
features.title: SCA (Software Composition Analysis)

features.description: Scans container images and configurations for security issues before deployment.
features.title: Container Security

features.description: Identifies hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code repositories.
features.title: Secrets Detection

features.description: Scans infrastructure-as-code templates for misconfigurations.
features.title: IaC Security

features.description: Discovers and tests APIs for vulnerabilities across the software supply chain.
features.title: API Security

features.description: Includes Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist agents, plus a Checkmarx MCP Server, to help developers fix findings faster.
features.title: AI-powered agents

pros: Broad scanning coverage spanning SAST, DAST, SCA, container, secrets, IaC, and API security in one platform
AI-powered remediation and triage agents aim to reduce manual fix time
Recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Software Supply Chain Security
Holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certification, and FedRAMP authorization
Reports over 1,800 enterprise customers and 40%+ of the Fortune 500 as users
cons: Pricing is fully custom and not published; requires a sales conversation to get a quote
No free trial is advertised on the site
Modular add-on structure (engines, AI agents, governance) can make total cost hard to estimate upfront

faqs.answer: Pricing is quote-based, calculated on developers, apps, and usage; a sales representative builds a custom proposal, typically provided within one business day.
faqs.question: How is Checkmarx One priced?

faqs.answer: The website does not advertise a free trial; pricing and access are handled through a custom sales quote.
faqs.question: Does Checkmarx offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: The platform includes NG SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, secrets detection, IaC security, API security, repository health, and malicious package protection, offered as modular components.
faqs.question: What scanning capabilities does Checkmarx One include?

faqs.answer: Yes, Checkmarx One includes AI-powered agents such as Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist, plus a Checkmarx MCP Server.
faqs.question: Does Checkmarx use AI?

faqs.answer: Checkmarx states it is used by over 1,800 enterprises and by more than 40% of the Fortune 500.
faqs.question: Is Checkmarx suitable for large enterprises?

vaultwarden.name: Vaultwarden
vaultwarden.short_description: Free, open-source Rust server that implements the Bitwarden Client API for self-hosted password management.

features.description: Implements the Bitwarden Client API in Rust, so official Bitwarden mobile, desktop, and browser clients can connect to it.
features.title: Bitwarden API compatibility

features.description: Store and manage personal passwords and vault items.
features.title: Personal vault management

features.description: Share text or files securely with the built-in Send feature.
features.title: Send

features.description: Supports organizations with collections, password sharing, member roles, and groups.
features.title: Organizations

features.description: Supports Authenticator apps, Email, FIDO2 WebAuthn, YubiKey, and Duo for MFA.
features.title: Multi-factor authentication

features.description: Grant trusted contacts emergency access to a vault.
features.title: Emergency access

features.description: Includes an admin backend interface for managing the self-hosted instance.
features.title: Admin backend

pros: Free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license
Compatible with official Bitwarden client apps across mobile, desktop, and browser
Written in Rust using the Rocket framework, described as lightweight for self-hosting
Supports strong MFA options including FIDO2 WebAuthn and YubiKey
Active community project with 63.8k GitHub stars
cons: Unofficial project, not affiliated with or officially supported by Bitwarden Inc.
Requires self-hosting and container knowledge (Docker/Podman) to deploy
Requires HTTPS with Web Crypto API support to function

faqs.answer: No, Vaultwarden is an unofficial, community-built alternative server implementation of the Bitwarden Client API, compatible with official Bitwarden clients.
faqs.question: Is Vaultwarden the same as Bitwarden?

faqs.answer: Yes, it's free, open-source software licensed under AGPL-3.0.
faqs.question: Is Vaultwarden free?

faqs.answer: Yes, it was formerly called Bitwarden_RS and was renamed in v1.21.0 to avoid trademark confusion.
faqs.question: Was Vaultwarden previously called something else?

faqs.answer: Official Bitwarden mobile, desktop, and browser clients.
faqs.question: What clients work with Vaultwarden?

fusionauth.name: FusionAuth
fusionauth.short_description: Customer identity and access management platform with a free self-hosted Community Edition and cloud plans from $162/month.

features.description: Supports multi-factor authentication and passkey-based passwordless login.

features.description: Passwordless login via magic links sent to users.

features.description: Supports SSO across applications along with social login providers.

features.description: Manage permissions across applications with role-based access control.

features.description: Built API-first, with webhooks for integrating authentication events into other systems.

features.title: SDKs and quickstarts

pros: Free Community Edition with core authentication features that can be self-hosted
Flexible deployment: self-hosted, cloud, on-premises, hybrid, or air-gapped
Broad SDK and quickstart support across many languages and frameworks
Usage-based cloud pricing intended to avoid surprise costs at scale, per the company
cons: Advanced features like breached password detection and application theming require paid plans
Essentials and Enterprise cloud plans start at $2,970/month
24/7 phone and email support is only available on the Enterprise plan

faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free to self-host with core authentication features.
faqs.question: Is FusionAuth free?

faqs.answer: The Starter cloud plan is $162 per month, billed annually.
faqs.question: What's the cheapest paid plan?

faqs.answer: Yes, all plans support self-hosting via ZIP, DEB, RPM, Docker, or Kubernetes.
faqs.question: Can FusionAuth be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan includes 24/7 phone and email support and a private Slack channel.

drupal-commerce.name: Drupal Commerce
drupal-commerce.short_description: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module for the Drupal CMS, maintained by Centarro since 2010.

features.description: Supports running multiple sites, languages, and currencies from a Drupal installation.

cons: Requires Drupal CMS knowledge to implement and maintain
